# 3. Step-by-Step Derivations & Analytical Matrix

## 3.1 Derivation 1 — Total DC Power Drawn from the Supplies

**Setup:** A complementary-symmetry Class AB output stage is powered from dual supplies $+V_{CC}$ and $-V_{CC}$. Each transistor is biased at quiescent current $I_Q$. For an input sinusoid large enough that $V_m \gg V_{BE(on)}$, the load current is approximately:

$$i_L(t) = I_m \sin(\omega t), \quad I_m = \frac{V_m}{R_L}$$

The NPN ($Q_1$) supplies current to the load during the positive half-cycle; the PNP ($Q_2$) sinks current during the negative half-cycle.

**Step 1 — Current drawn from the $+V_{CC}$ rail:**

$$i_{+}(t) = I_Q + i_L(t) \quad \text{for} \quad 0 < \omega t < \pi$$

$$i_{+}(t) = I_Q \quad \text{for} \quad \pi < \omega t < 2\pi$$

**Step 2 — Average current from $+V_{CC}$ over one full cycle:**

$$\begin{aligned}
I_{dc,+} &= \frac{1}{2\pi} \left[\int_0^{\pi} \left(I_Q + I_m \sin\theta\right) d\theta + \int_{\pi}^{2\pi} I_Q \, d\theta \right] \\[6pt]
&= \frac{1}{2\pi} \left[I_Q \cdot 2\pi + I_m \int_0^{\pi} \sin\theta \, d\theta \right] \\[6pt]
&= I_Q + \frac{I_m}{2\pi} \cdot \big[-\cos\theta\big]_0^{\pi} \\[6pt]
&= I_Q + \frac{I_m}{2\pi} \cdot (1+1) \\[6pt]
&= I_Q + \frac{I_m}{\pi}
\end{aligned}$$

**Step 3 — By symmetry, the $-V_{CC}$ rail draws the same average current:**

$$I_{dc,-} = I_Q + \frac{I_m}{\pi}$$

**Step 4 — Total DC power consumed from both supplies:**

$$\begin{aligned}
P_{DC} &= V_{CC} \cdot I_{dc,+} + V_{CC} \cdot I_{dc,-} \\[4pt]
&= V_{CC}\!\left(I_Q + \frac{I_m}{\pi}\right) + V_{CC}\!\left(I_Q + \frac{I_m}{\pi}\right) \\[4pt]
&= 2V_{CC}\!\left(I_Q + \frac{I_m}{\pi}\right) \\[4pt]
P_{DC} &= 2V_{CC}\, I_Q + \frac{2V_{CC}\, V_m}{\pi R_L}
\end{aligned}$$

> [!NOTE]
> The first term $2V_{CC}I_Q$ represents **wasted** power that exists even at zero signal input — this is the "Class AB tax" paid to suppress crossover distortion.

---

## 3.2 Derivation 2 — AC Output Power Delivered to the Load

For a sinusoidal output $v_o(t) = V_m \sin(\omega t)$ across load $R_L$:

$$\begin{aligned}
P_{ac} &= \frac{1}{T}\int_0^T \frac{v_o^2(t)}{R_L}\, dt \\[6pt]
&= \frac{1}{R_L}\cdot \frac{V_m^2}{T}\int_0^T \sin^2(\omega t)\, dt \\[6pt]
&= \frac{V_m^2}{R_L}\cdot \frac{1}{2} \\[6pt]
P_{ac} &= \frac{V_m^2}{2R_L} = \frac{I_m^2 R_L}{2}
\end{aligned}$$

**Maximum AC power** (when the output swing reaches the rail, $V_m \to V_{CC}$):

$$P_{ac,max} = \frac{V_{CC}^2}{2R_L}$$

---

## 3.3 Derivation 3 — Conversion Efficiency $\eta$

Dividing $P_{ac}$ by $P_{DC}$:

$$\begin{aligned}
\eta &= \frac{P_{ac}}{P_{DC}} = \frac{V_m^2 / (2R_L)}{2V_{CC}(I_Q + V_m/(\pi R_L))} \\[6pt]
&= \frac{V_m^2 / (2R_L)}{(2V_{CC} I_Q) + (2V_{CC} V_m)/(\pi R_L)} \\[6pt]
&= \frac{V_m^2}{4 V_{CC} I_Q R_L + (4V_{CC} V_m)/\pi} \\[6pt]
&= \frac{V_m^2/V_{CC}}{4 I_Q R_L + (4V_m)/\pi} \cdot \frac{1}{V_{CC}} \quad \text{(rearranged)} \\[6pt]
\eta &= \frac{(\pi/4)\,(V_m/V_{CC})}{1 + \pi I_Q R_L / V_m}
\end{aligned}$$

**Special cases:**

- If $I_Q \to 0$ (Class B limit), the denominator $\to 1$, recovering $\eta = (\pi/4)(V_m/V_{CC})$.
- If $V_m \to V_{CC}$, $\eta_{max} = \dfrac{\pi/4}{1 + \pi I_Q R_L / V_{CC}}$, which approaches **78.5 %** as $I_Q \to 0$.
- If $V_m \to 0$ (small-signal), $\eta \to 0$ (no useful output, but DC power $2V_{CC}I_Q$ is still being drawn).

---

## 3.4 Derivation 4 — Power Dissipated in the Output Transistors

By conservation of energy, the difference between the DC power supplied and the AC power delivered must be dissipated as heat in the transistors:

$$P_D = P_{DC} - P_{ac} = 2V_{CC} I_Q + \frac{2V_{CC} V_m}{\pi R_L} - \frac{V_m^2}{2R_L}$$

To find the **worst-case dissipation** (for heat-sink design), differentiate with respect to $V_m$ and set to zero:

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dP_D}{dV_m} &= \frac{2V_{CC}}{\pi R_L} - \frac{V_m}{R_L} = 0 \\[6pt]
\Rightarrow V_m^* &= \frac{2V_{CC}}{\pi}
\end{aligned}$$

Substituting back:

$$P_{D,max} = 2V_{CC}I_Q + \frac{2V_{CC}}{\pi R_L}\cdot\frac{2V_{CC}}{\pi} - \frac{1}{2R_L}\!\left(\frac{2V_{CC}}{\pi}\right)^{\!2} = 2V_{CC}I_Q + \frac{2V_{CC}^2}{\pi^2 R_L}$$

Ignoring the small $I_Q$ contribution:

$$\boxed{\,P_{D,max} \approx \frac{2V_{CC}^2}{\pi^2 R_L} \approx \frac{V_{CC}^2}{5 R_L}\,}$$

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This worst case occurs at $V_m = 2V_{CC}/\pi \approx 0.637\,V_{CC}$, *not* at full power. Heat-sink design for a Class AB amplifier **must** be sized for this intermediate level — a common KTU exam pitfall!

---

## 3.5 Numerical Example (Worked Problem)

**Problem:** A Class AB output stage operates from $\pm 15\text{ V}$ supplies, drives $R_L = 8\ \Omega$, and has a quiescent current $I_Q = 20\text{ mA}$ per transistor. For a sinusoidal output of peak amplitude $V_m = 10\text{ V}$:

**(a) Total DC power drawn:**

$$\begin{aligned}
P_{DC} &= 2(15)\!\left(0.020 + \frac{10}{\pi \cdot 8}\right) \\
&= 30 \!\left(0.020 + 0.3979\right) \\
&= 30 \times 0.4179 \\
P_{DC} &\approx 12.54\ \text{W}
\end{aligned}$$

**(b) AC output power:**

$$P_{ac} = \frac{(10)^2}{2 \times 8} = \frac{100}{16} = 6.25\ \text{W}$$

**(c) Conversion efficiency:**

$$\eta = \frac{6.25}{12.54} \approx 49.8\,\%$$

**(d) Power dissipated in transistors (heat):**

$$P_D = 12.54 - 6.25 = 6.29\ \text{W} \quad (\approx 3.15\ \text{W per device})$$

> [!NOTE]
> Notice the efficiency is far below the 78.5 % maximum — because $I_Q$ (20 mA) is non-negligible compared to $I_m = 10/8 = 1.25\text{ A}$. At full output swing ($V_m = 15\text{ V}$), the efficiency climbs above 70 %.

# 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Block-Level Functional Architecture — Class AB Output Stage

The diagram below maps the **functional blocks and signal flow** of a complete Class AB push-pull power amplifier, from input through bias generation to thermal load. All node IDs are alphanumeric and labels are pure uppercase text to comply with Mermaid safeguards.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[INPUT SIGNAL SOURCE] --> B[INPUT DRIVER STAGE]
    B --> C[VBE MULTIPLIER BIAS NETWORK]
    C --> D[UPPER OUTPUT TRANSISTOR Q1 NPN]
    C --> E[LOWER OUTPUT TRANSISTOR Q2 PNP]
    D --> F[OUTPUT NODE VO]
    E --> F
    F --> G[LOAD RESISTOR RL]
    F --> H[FEEDBACK NETWORK TO DRIVER]
    H --> B
    P[POSITIVE SUPPLY PLUS VCC] --> D
    N[NEGATIVE SUPPLY MINUS VCC] --> E
    D --> Q1H[HEATSINK Q1]
    E --> Q2H[HEATSINK Q2]
    C --> T1[THERMAL COUPLING TO OUTPUT DEVICES]
    D --> T1
    E --> T1
```

**Interpretation of the block flow:**

1. The **input driver** (often a VAS — voltage amplifier stage) provides sufficient base drive to the output devices.
2. The **$V_{BE}$ multiplier** generates the small bias voltage $V_{BB}$ between the bases of $Q_1$ and $Q_2$.
3. **$Q_1$ and $Q_2$** operate as a complementary emitter-follower pair, alternately sourcing and sinking load current.
4. The **output node** drives the load $R_L$ and feeds back a portion of the signal to the driver for linearity and gain setting.
5. **Thermal coupling** ensures the multiplier's $V_{BE3}$ tracks the output transistors' $V_{BE}$, maintaining bias stability across temperature.

---

## 4.2 Mermaid Flowchart — Class AB Biasing Decision Logic

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    S[START ANALYZE AMPLIFIER CLASS] --> Q1{IS IQ ZERO AT REST}
    Q1 -- YES --> B[CLASS B CONDUCTION 180 DEG]
    Q1 -- NO --> Q2{IS IQ LARGE IDLE CURRENT}
    Q2 -- YES --> A[CLASS A CONDUCTION 360 DEG]
    Q2 -- NO --> AB[CLASS AB CONDUCTION 180 TO 360 DEG]
    B -- DISTORTED --> FIX1[ADD VBB BIAS GREATER THAN 2VBE]
    FIX1 --> AB
    A -- INEFFICIENT --> FIX2[REDUCE BIAS TO MINIMUM VIABLE IQ]
    FIX2 --> AB
    AB --> OUT[COMPROMISE NO CROSSOVER DISTORTION HIGH EFFICIENCY]
```

---

## 4.3 Mermaid Sequence — Thermal Stability via $V_{BE}$ Multiplier

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant TEMP as AMBIENT TEMPERATURE
    participant Q3 as VBE MULTIPLIER TRANSISTOR
    participant Q1 as UPPER OUTPUT NPN
    participant Q2 as LOWER OUTPUT PNP
    TEMP-->>Q1: Temperature rises
    TEMP-->>Q2: Temperature rises
    TEMP-->>Q3: Temperature rises
    Q3->>Q1: VBE3 drops by 2 mV per degree C
    Q3->>Q2: Bias voltage VBB drops accordingly
    Q1->>Q1: Reduced base drive limits IQ rise
    Q2->>Q2: Reduced base drive limits IQ rise
    Note over Q1,Q2: THERMAL RUNAWAY PREVENTED
```
